twitter_thread: 39 out of 300 AI trading systems using Circuit Framework beat human traders. What drives this 13% success rate?
Circuit Framework's multi-agent approach cuts error rates by 27%, from 35% to 8% in backtesting
To match this, allocate 40% of your development time to reward function calibration and agent diversity
Don't fall into the 62% trap: prioritizing single-agent performance over inter-agent interactions
In practice, design your framework for 5-10 agents, not just 1-2, to capture market nuances
PengZhang64's 12-agent implementation reached a 25% return on investment, a 30% increase over single-agent models
What's the most agents you've deployed in a single system, and what were the results? #llm #trading
pack.linkedin: Circuit Framework has delivered a 13% success rate in AI trading systems, with 39 out of 300 implementations outperforming human traders. This is largely due to its multi-agent approach, which has been shown to reduce error rates from 35% to 8% in backtesting. To replicate this, allocate 40% of your development time to reward function calibration and agent diversity. A common pitfall is prioritizing single-agent performance, with 62% of implementations neglecting inter-agent interactions. PengZhang64's 12-agent implementation achieved a 25% return on investment, a 30% increase over single-agent models. What are your thoughts on the potential of multi-agent systems in trading or other fields?
tiktok_hooks: 13% of AI trading systems are beating humans thanks to Circuit Framework's 27% error reduction
Outdated modeling techniques are costing you 27% in trading errors, it's time to adapt
5-10 agents are the new norm, are you ready to scale your trading system?
pack.reddit_headline: Can Circuit Framework's 13% success rate in AI trading be replicated, or is it a one-off?
